scope: parent_id set null and children_ids replaced on re-derive
summary: deriveGraphMetadata always overwrites parent_id and fully replaces children_ids. mergeGraphMetadata spreads refreshed and does not preserve top-level parent_id or children_ids, so a re-derive can undo an append-only save and erase valid lineage. Confirmed against live code.

title: Preserve parent_id when re-derivation is ambiguous
problem: deriveGraphMetadata always overwrites parent_id with resolveParentId, which returns null whenever the immediate parent segment is not a numeric spec leaf. mergeGraphMetadata preserves manual and selected derived fields but not parent_id, so the merge accepts a null and discards a valid existing parent.
evidence: deriveGraphMetadata graph-metadata-parser.ts:1129, resolveParentId :669-679 returns null, mergeGraphMetadata :1149-1161 spreads refreshed without preserving parent_id
proposal: In mergeGraphMetadata reconcile parent_id as refreshed.parent_id ?? existing?.parent_id ?? null, with a review flag when they disagree. Only clear an existing parent in an explicit prune or repair mode, and verify the referenced parent metadata exists before preserving.

title: Make children_ids append-only by default
problem: deriveGraphMetadata fully replaces children_ids via resolveChildrenIds which scans direct directories and returns a sorted replacement. mergeGraphMetadata does not union or preserve existing children, so a scan that is incomplete or racing another session deletes children.
evidence: resolveChildrenIds graph-metadata-parser.ts:681-690, mergeGraphMetadata :1149-1161, append-only canonical path updatePhaseParentPointer generate-context.ts:575-579
proposal: Move the append or union behavior into the merge path: default children_ids = stableUnion(existing, refreshed). Full pruning should require an explicit pruneChildren or repair operation.

title: Scope backfill to one folder before touching parent or children
problem: backfill exposes only --root, not a true single-folder target, then collectSpecFolders refreshes every discovered folder, so a scoped operation rewrites sibling parents children_ids.
proposal: Add --folder or positional folder support that resolves one packet and refreshes only that packet plus optionally a surgical parent append. Keep --root as explicit tree mode. Overlaps with a1-f1, merge into the scoped boundary.

title: Unify the phase-child contract used by parser and save path
problem: isPhaseParent accepts a child with spec.md OR description.json, isSpecLeafSegment accepts broader names including underscores and bare numerics, and resolveChildrenIds only checks directory name. The three disagree on what a child is.
evidence: isPhaseParent is-phase-parent.ts:5-8 and :39-53, isSpecLeafSegment spec-doc-paths.ts:65-67, resolveChildrenIds graph-metadata-parser.ts:681-686
proposal: Expose one shared listPhaseChildren helper used by both isPhaseParent and resolveChildrenIds. Add regression tests for existing non-null parent with derived null, children preserved when scan returns empty, and append-only save followed by backfill.
